parsed_review.summary: CP00-908 binds the RP30 HRX AttendanceWorkflow tail (RP30.P02.M02.S03-RP30.P02.M02.S12, risk A, 10 implementation units) as descriptor-only evidence. Directly verified via Read/Grep/Glob: pack identity, range, unit_count, and binding chain (CP907->CP908->CP909, next_subphase RP30.P02.M02.S13) are consistent across manifest, review-baseline, live closeout-pack-plan.json, HRX_CP908_PACK_BINDING (registry.js:270), the generated contract, and the validate script (140-148). The descriptor is built from deep-frozen rows with every attendance runtime/write/real-data flag set false (registry.js:1017-1022) and spreads HRX_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION (registry.js:2622); validators.js:1906-2015 rejects any drift in no-write, no-real-data, attendance-runtime-closed, authority-boundary, hermes/claude packet, capability, safety-gate, and no-leak-guard fields, and validateHrxPeopleContract pins latest_pack=CP00-908 (validators.js:2022-2023). Grep across packages/hrx/src and the validate script found zero write/exec/spawn/fetch/http/child_process primitives, confirming pure metadata + read-only validation. Three CP908 tests assert errors==[] and the descriptor-only boundaries (test/model.test.js:643-700). Authority boundary is honored: Claude is read-only reviewer (not final approver), Hermes packet emits_runtime_receipt=false (deterministic review evidence, not product runtime), and the manifest honestly claims production_ready=false, runtime_ready=false, descriptor_only, opens_runtime=false, writes_product_state=false, uses_real_client_data=false with no runtime or enterprise-trust over-claim. The pending adjudication/inspection/claude-review-result are expected placeholders awaiting this required review. One non-blocking informational finding: command-evidence.json records only an initial 6-command subset and self-notes the full closeout validation ladder + git diff --check must be re-run by the deterministic harness before commit, which a read-only reviewer cannot execute. No P0/P1/P2 findings; the hrx_attendance_workflow_tail_descriptor_verified claim is well-supported.

findings.title: Full closeout validation ladder pending re-execution before commit (outside read-only purview)
findings.details: command-evidence.json records only an initial 6-command subset (hrx_syntax_check_initial, hrx_model_test_initial, rp30:hrx:validate, rp30:validate, hrx:requirements:validate, hrx:weighted:validate), all exit_code 0, and its notes (line 49-52) explicitly state the full validation ladder must be re-run before commit. The prompt's required closeout ladder (npm run closeout-pack-plan:validate, closeout-pack:validate, npm test, validate, spec:requirements:validate, weighted:validate, fullplan:validate, goal:closeout:validate, rp00:control-plane:validate, git diff --check) is not yet recorded as executed. As a read-only reviewer I cannot run Bash, so this final deterministic-harness gate evidence is pending and could not be directly confirmed. Implementation, descriptor, validator, and test inspection are all sound, so this is an evidence-provenance note, not an implementation defect.
findings.recommendation: Before commit, have the deterministic harness execute the full validation ladder and git diff --check, capturing exit codes into command-evidence.json, so the closeout receipt reflects the complete gate rather than the initial subset. No implementation change required.
findings.post_review_disposition: Non-blocking informational. By design this ladder is the deterministic harness's responsibility prior to commit (per command-evidence.json note), outside the read-only reviewer's purview; does not block pack or goal closeout.
